history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible at row 05, column F, two cells ahead and two cells to the right of my location. The cell directly in front of me (north) is lava (L), so I cannot move forward. The cell to my right (east) is a safe floor (.), while the cell to my left (west) is a wall (#). I will turn clockwise to face the safe floor cell to the east and begin navigating around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 7, column D, facing north. The cell immediately ahead (north) is a safe floor (.). The cell to the left (west) is lava (L), and the cell to the right (east) is a wall (#). The goal is at row 5, column B. To reach it, the agent must first move forward into the safe cell at row 6, column D, then turn left to head west through the safe corridor at row 6, columns C and B, and finally move north to the goal. Since moving forward is the only safe immediate action, I will do that now.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at D07, facing north. The goal is at B06, two cells west and one cell north. The

<thought>
The agent is at D07 facing north. The cell directly ahead (D06) is safe floor, but it's a dead end because north is a wall, west is lava, and east is a wall. The goal is at B06. To reach it safely, the agent must go west: first to C07, then to B07, then north to B06. Since the agent is facing north, it should turn left to face west.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at D07 facing north. The cell directly ahead, D06, is safe floor. The path to the goal at E05 is: go north to D06, then north to D05, then east to E05. Since I'm already facing north, moving forward is the correct first step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead (row 6, column D) is a safe floor cell. The cells to the left and right are lava, so turning is dangerous. Moving forward will place me adjacent to the goal. From there, I can turn and reach the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 7 column D facing north. The cell in front of me is a wall (#). The goal is at row 7 column E, which is immediately to my right (east). I need to turn right to face the goal, then move forward to reach it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 7, column D, currently facing east after the previous TURN_RIGHT. The goal (G) is at row 6, column D, directly to my north (left relative to my current eastward orientation). The cell directly ahead of me (row 7, column E) is safe floor, but moving there would put me adjacent to lava at row 6, column E. The immediate safe path to the goal is to turn left to face north and then move forward. Therefore, I will turn left now.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 7, column D, facing north. The cell ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal (G) is at row 7, column E, immediately to my right. I will turn right to face east, putting the goal in front of me for a forward move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
